title: Sweet Springy Jamaican Milk Pudding
description: A malty, springy Jamaican dessert featuring milk, pickle, and a touch of allspice. Perfect for special occasions or a springtime treat.

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: whole milk or a lactose-free alternative

ingredients.name: pickle juice
ingredients.notes: use a mild pickle juice for best flavor

ingredients.name: allspice
ingredients.notes: ground allspice, can be adjusted to taste

ingredients.name: cornstarch
ingredients.notes: make sure to mix well with milk before adding to pudding

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine milk, canned milk, pickle juice, allspice, and vanilla extract. Whisk until smooth and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
In a small bowl, mix cornstarch with a splash of milk until smooth. Add the cornstarch mixture to the saucepan and whisk until well combined.
Continue to cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the pudding thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly, then pour into a cataplana or individual ramekins to serve.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the pudding to set and develop its springy texture.
Serve chilled, garnished with a sprinkle of allspice or a few fresh mint leaves, if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
